revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} [[File:Obihiro-jinja.jpg|thumb|]] '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its annual festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. ==Overview== {{Shrine | Name=Obihiro Shrine | Image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | Location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | Coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | Deities=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | Rank=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | Founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | Main Hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Annual Festival=[[September 24]] | Map={{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, Obihiro Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Obihiro]], [[Hokkaido]]. Its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. The shrine enshrines the Three Deities of Cultivation: [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ōnamuchi no Kami]], and [[Sukunabikona no Kami]]. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:History of Obihiro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Dd]] [[Category:Dd]][[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}}

In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:History of Obihiro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}}

The shrine enshrines the Three Deities of Cultivation: [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ōnamuchi no Kami]], and [[Sukunabikona no Kami]]. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). {{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} [[File:Obihiro-jinja.jpg|thumb|]] '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, Obihiro Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Obihiro]], [[Hokkaido]]. Its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. The shrine enshrines the Three Deities of Cultivation: [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ōnamuchi no Kami]], and [[Sukunabikona no Kami]]. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:History of Obihiro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}}

The shrine enshrines the Three Deities of Cultivation: [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ōnamuchi no Kami]], and [[Sukunabikona no Kami]]. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:History of Obihiro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Beppyo Shrines]] ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]][[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia21]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}}

Its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. The shrine enshrines the Three Deities of Cultivation: [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ōnamuchi no Kami]], and [[Sukunabikona no Kami]]. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. The shrine enshrines the Three Deities of Cultivation: [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ōnamuchi no Kami]], and [[Sukunabikona no Kami]]. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:History of Obihiro]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[Category:dardar]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}}

Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, Obihiro Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Obihiro]], [[Hokkaido]]. Its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. The shrine enshrines the Three Deities of Cultivation: [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ōnamuchi no Kami]], and [[Sukunabikona no Kami]]. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, Obihiro Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Obihiro]], [[Hokkaido]]. Its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. The shrine enshrines the Three Deities of Cultivation: [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ōnamuchi no Kami]], and [[Sukunabikona no Kami]]. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, Obihiro Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Obihiro]], [[Hokkaido]]. Its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. The shrine enshrines the Three Deities of Cultivation: [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ōnamuchi no Kami]], and [[Sukunabikona no Kami]]. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * {{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} [[File:Obihiro-jinja.jpg|thumb|]] '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]

[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, Obihiro Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Obihiro]], [[Hokkaido]]. Its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. The shrine enshrines the Three Deities of Cultivation: [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ōnamuchi no Kami]], and [[Sukunabikona no Kami]]. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} [[File:Obihiro-jinja.jpg|thumb|]] '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, Obihiro Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Obihiro]], [[Hokkaido]]. Its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was Prefectural Shrine. The shrine enshrines the Three Deities of Cultivation: [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]], [[Ōnamuchi no Kami]], and [[Sukunabikona no Kami]]. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from {{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * {{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. {{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from revision.comment: Text replacement - "qqq" to "qq"
revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from {{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from {{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from {{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * {{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:History of Obihiro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Obihiro Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Obihiro Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] [[sn:Obihiro Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obihiro Shrine}}

{{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:History of Obihiro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Obihiro Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[sn:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]]

In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[Hokkaido]][[Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:History of Obihiro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[sn:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]
