revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11481193]] Wikidata name: Obihiro Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=[[File:Obihiro Shrine P2.jpg|270px|Obihiro Shrine haiden]]
Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E}}}} | location=[[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]][[:en:Obihiro|Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{coord|42|55|55.88|N|143|12|31.58|E|display=title,inline}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[:en:September 24|September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Obihiro, Hokkaido|Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[:en:Hokkaido Shrine|Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[:en:1918|1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1930|1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[:en:Silver Spoon|Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[sn:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]]

Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. By [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[:en:Hokkaido Shrine|Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[:en:1918|1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1930|1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[:en:Silver Spoon|Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] {{wikidata link|Q11481193}}

[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[:en:September 24|September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | prefecture={{ill|Obihiro|WD=Q177149}} | architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Obihiro, Hokkaido|Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[:en:Hokkaido Shrine|Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[:en:1918|1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1930|1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[:en:Silver Spoon|Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] {{wikidata link|Q11481193}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[:en:September 24|September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | prefecture={{ill|Obihiro|WD=Q177149}} | architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Obihiro, Hokkaido|Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[:en:Hokkaido Shrine|Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[:en:1918|1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1930|1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[:en:Silver Spoon|Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] {{wikidata link|Q11481193}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[commons:Category:Obihiro-jinja]][[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[ja:帯廣神社]][[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] [[commons:Category:Obihiro-jinja]][[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[ja:帯廣神社]][[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] [[sn:commons:Category:Obihiro-jinja]][[en:Obihiro Shrine]][[ja:帯廣神社]][[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11481193]] Wikidata name: Obihiro Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Obihiro, Hokkaido, Japan}} {{unreferenced|date=December 2021}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name=Obihiro Shrine | image=File:Obihiro-jinja.jpg | location=[[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]][[:en:Obihiro|Obihiro]] Higashi 3-jō Minami 2-chōme 1-ban | coordinates={{Coord|42.93214|N|143.20884|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi no Kami]]
[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded=[[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival=[[:en:September 24|September 24]] | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | prefecture={{ill|Obihiro|WD=Q177149}} | architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Obihiro, Hokkaido|Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In [[:en:1883|1883]] (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around [[:en:1885|1885]] (Meiji 18). By [[:en:1890|1890]] (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In [[:en:1909|1909]] (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[:en:Hokkaido Shrine|Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in [[:en:1918|1918]] (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1930|1930]] (Shōwa 5). In [[:en:1976|1976]] (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[:en:Silver Spoon|Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] {{wikidata link|Q11481193}} [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} [[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] {{wikidata link|Q11481193}} [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Obihiro Shrine was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883}} (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885}} (Meiji 18). By {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909}} (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910}} (Meiji 43). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in {{ill|1911|en|1911|WD=Q2076|lt=1911}} (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in {{ill|1918|en|1918|WD=Q2094|lt=1918}} (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930}} (Shōwa 5). In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[:en:Silver Spoon|Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] {{wikidata link|Q11481193}} [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded={{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910}} (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival={{ill|September 24|en|September 24|WD=Q2890|lt=September 24}} | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | prefecture={{ill|Obihiro|WD=Q177149}} | architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Obihiro, Hokkaido|Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883}} (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885}} (Meiji 18). By {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909}} (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910}} (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[:en:Hokkaido Shrine|Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in {{ill|1911|en|1911|WD=Q2076|lt=1911}} (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in {{ill|1918|en|1918|WD=Q2094|lt=1918}} (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930}} (Shōwa 5). In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[:en:Silver Spoon|Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] {{wikidata link|Q11481193}} [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[Ōnamuchi no Kami]]
[[Sukunabikona no Kami]] | type=Prefectural Shrine, Beppyo Shrine | founded={{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910}} (Meiji 43) | main hall=[[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival={{ill|September 24|en|September 24|WD=Q2890|lt=September 24}} | map={{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=42|lat_min=55|lat_sec=55.8|lon_deg=143|lon_min=12|lon_sec=31.5|label=Obihiro Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|Alternativemap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} | prefecture={{ill|Obihiro|WD=Q177149}} | architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Obihiro Shrine''' (帯廣神社, ''Obihiro jinja'') is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Obihiro, Hokkaido|Obihiro, Hokkaido]]. Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. Obihiro Shrine was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. == History == In {{ill|1883|en|1883|WD=Q7816|lt=1883}} (Meiji 16), the pioneering group "Bansei-sha" relocated to Obihiro and started performing rites around {{ill|1885|en|1885|WD=Q7821|lt=1885}} (Meiji 18). By {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890}} (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909}} (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910}} (Meiji 43). On September 24th of the same year, spirits were transferred from Sapporo Shrine (now [[:en:Hokkaido Shrine|Hokkaido Shrine]]). The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in {{ill|1911|en|1911|WD=Q2076|lt=1911}} (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in {{ill|1918|en|1918|WD=Q2094|lt=1918}} (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930}} (Shōwa 5). In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[:en:Silver Spoon|Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] {{wikidata link|Q11481193}} [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

The shrine was designated as a Mukakusha (unranked shrine) in {{ill|1911|en|1911|WD=Q2076|lt=1911|1=1911}} (Meiji 44), elevated to Gōsha (village shrine) in {{ill|1918|en|1918|WD=Q2094|lt=1918|1=1918}} (Taishō 7), and further promoted to Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1930|en|1930|WD=Q18787|lt=1930|1=1930}} (Shōwa 5). In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[:en:Silver Spoon|Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] {{wikidata link|Q11481193}} [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

Erected in 1910, it is dedicated to the [[kami]] Ōkuni-mitama no mikoto (大國魂神), [[Ōkuninushi]] no mikoto (大那牟遲神), and [[Sukunabikona]] no mikoto (少彦名神). Its festival is on September 24. By {{ill|1890|en|1890|WD=Q7832|lt=1890|1=1890}} (Meiji 23), a small shrine was built and called "Obihiro Shrine." This is considered the origin of the current shrine. However, Obihiro Shrine acknowledges that the relationship between Bansei-sha's "Obihiro Shrine" and the present shrine is unclear. In {{ill|1909|en|1909|WD=Q2057|lt=1909|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), a decision was made to establish a new shrine at the current location, and a shrine establishment petition was submitted to the Hokkaido Government in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910|1=1910}} (Meiji 43). In {{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), it was included in the list of Beppyo Shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines. == In Popular Culture == * The manga and anime series [[:en:Silver Spoon|Silver Spoon]] features "Dai Ezo Shrine," modeled after Obihiro Shrine. It is depicted as the setting for the protagonist and heroine's first date. == External Links == * [http://www.obihirojinja.jp/ Obihiro Shrine] (official site) * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/14/14001.html Obihiro Shrine] (Hokkaido Shrine Association) {{shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan#Hokkaid.C5.8D|List of Shinto shrines in Hokkaidō]] ==External links== *[http://www.obihirojinja.jp Official website] *[https://hokkaidojinjacho.jp/帯廣神社/ Hokkaido Shinto listing] {{coord|42.93214|143.20884|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[simple:Obihiro Shrine]] [[en:Obihiro Shrine]] {{Commons category|Obihiro-jinja}} [[ja:帯廣神社]] [[mr:ओबिहिरो तीर्थ]] {{wikidata link|Q11481193}} [[Category:1910 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Obihiro]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
